short_rationale: Typical 2-hop bridge from an anchor entity to a target attribute, with some numeric endpoints.
Two-hop bridge questions that end in a date or time-valued fact.
Straightforward two-entity lookup after one bridge step.
Dense multi-entity chains with temporal targets and substantial grounding load.
Common who-after-bridge pattern with minimal extra constraints.
Multi-entity who-questions requiring grounding several linked mentions before lookup.
Long chained lookups with name matching, historical variants, or language-variant resolution.
Dense long chains that terminate in numeric or statistical facts.
Long multi-entity who-questions, with occasional multi-party answer construction.
Rare mix of dense temporal chains with superlative or exclusion-style selection constraints.
Two-hop person lookup via a simple country or place bridge.
Dense what-questions requiring multiple linked entity resolutions before final attribute lookup.
Two-hop bridge via a country, then a role-holder lookup.
Rare 4-hop dense chains with border-style constraints and some numeric endpoints.
Common 2-hop bridge lookups, often returning descriptive span answers.
Dense named entities, but mostly simple country or region to person retrieval.
Multi-entity bridge questions targeting first or earliest dated events.
Very common short 2-hop lookup pattern with concise answers.
Dense multi-entity bridges mixing place grounding with population or region-value lookup.
Two-hop questions resolved through historical or time-anchored entity retrieval.
Dense-entity 2-hop lookups across people, places, wars, and media roles.
Mostly straightforward creator or performer bridges to affiliation or location facts.
Hard 3-hop dense chains with spatial constraints and occasional numeric attributes.
Long multi-entity temporal questions with first or last comparisons and alias grounding.
2-hop pairwise questions use a first/earliest bridge before a final attribute lookup.
Dense 3-hop chains require grounding several linked entities before the final lookup.
Location-focused 3-hop compositions stress multi-entity grounding across linked places and organizations.
Mostly 3-hop relation chains over two anchors, ending in a final property lookup.
A bridged location and time anchor jointly constrain the answer entity, a useful rare pattern.
Straightforward 2-hop bridge from an entity to a numeric attribute or count.
Short-hop items still stress dense named-entity grounding before the final lookup.
Rare 4-hop questions combine dense multi-entity grounding, superlatives, and date answers.
Very long 4-hop dense-entity compositions require several intermediate bridges and synthesis.
Includes rare first/only and intersection-style constraints after a short bridge.
2-hop questions still involve multiple named entities or institutions before the answer lookup.
Pairwise 2-hop items often use first/last temporal cues or analogous bridged constraints.
Dense 2-hop bridges from pop-culture or geography clues to a person, often with time-sensitive grounding.
Rare 3-hop imperial-history chain: polity to ruler at greatest extent to the ruler's mother.
Multi-entity questions resolve a country, then recover a time-anchored officeholder or last-executed person.
Very long 4-hop compositions mixing organizations, countries, intersections, and temporal anchors.
Straight 2-hop attribute chaining from one seed entity.
Simple team-to-namesake lookups with city and state style outputs.
2-hop multi-entity bridges from song or place clues to a role-holder or advocacy group.
Nested 3-hop dense lookups over film, actor, and place clues.
2-hop grounding followed by a date or ranked numeric attribute.
Dense 3-hop questions that bridge to a country or city and retrieve counts, amounts, or durations.
Very long chains with superlatives and time anchors, usually ending in a year lookup.
Counts over filtered sets defined by continent, organization, ethnicity, or residence constraints.
Two-hop bridge via birthplace/state/city to a second fact such as a namesake or originator.
Dense entity mentions, but the core task is a 2-hop bridge from work/person to country and then to a location.
Three-step organizational chain from place to country to institution to organ, filtered by the since-1994 condition.
Four-hop nested geographic or event chains ending in a date lookup, with strong temporal filtering.
Very long dense-entity chains with films, actors, places, and geographic relations composed across 4 hops.
Dense multi-entity chain anchored by 2012 and 2018 dates before resolving the final country attribute.
Straightforward 2-hop geographic composition from place to county to adjacent body of water.
Requires grounding dense entities and computing age or duration from temporally anchored facts.
Single-anchor historical questions depend on temporal ordering or era resolution before a second lookup.
Player-to-team-to-title-season-to-manager chain with a modest temporal anchor.
Rare same-country or same-state constraint pattern requiring cross-entity attribute comparison and filtering.
Bridge from sub-location or habitat to a parent island or territory, with some members needing explanatory answers.
Cross-work and name-match bridging over dense entities, ending in a short entity answer.
Very long 4-hop chains with indirect country bridging and year/date resolution.
Dense multi-entity location questions anchored by before/after movement or historical relocation.
Two-hop bridge from a referenced branch or namesake city to a time-constrained officeholder.
Canonical 2-hop relation chaining with minimal extra constraints.
Single-anchor questions mix superlative or ordinal selection with historical time filtering.
Rare dense 4-hop same-conflict comparison requiring multiple grounded entities before year lookup.
Three-hop dense descriptions rely on indirect location or name bridges to reach the final entity.
Mostly short 2-hop lookups with ordinal, initial-name, or original-name constraints.
Indirect 3-hop questions combine superlative or winner selection with institutional or regional lookup.
Dense 3-hop chains through film people and cities, sometimes ending in historical year retrieval.
Long 4-hop history and geography chains with temporal anchoring and indirect area identification.
Dense 3-hop question with same-religion comparison, state bridging, and a final date lookup.
Party matching bridges from Charles Manatt to the first same-party president and then to a spouse.
Requires chaining across film and TV through the shared actor identity.
Uses the broadcaster behind Bagpuss as a bridge to an album title and then its band.
A 4-hop multi-entity chain with shared-college matching and a founder constraint.
Finds a continent via tournament and host facts, then retrieves the time of continental collision.
Bridges from a book to its central biblical figure and then to a descriptive death account.
Straight two-hop lookup from serviceman to military branch to founder.
Pure relation-chain example from film to director to attended graduate school.
Complex multi-entity question bridges through tour geography and filters for an explorer tied to two regions.
Birthplace bridging plus a three-country homeland constraint makes this a strong set-based case.
Long relation chain maps a historical figure to a game title and then its development duration.
4-hop dense chain crossing album performer, lyricist, label, and genre.
Bridges from a Hoover-related agency to the earliest disclosed budget year.
Need singer-to-birthplace bridging plus superlative event selection.
Filters presidents by a unique family relation, then retrieves the father.
Org headquarters city is bridged to a seasonal time expression.
Uses channel-name matching to disambiguate the correct Today show before broadcaster lookup.
Find the historical period, then its starting area within a Jacobinism-affected continent.
Simple temporal lookup via person to birthplace to fall date.
Bridges from a work to its performer, then retrieves a hit-count total.
Uses Bay of Pigs location to identify the owner tied to New York World.
Player to team to namesake-city lookup with a list-like location answer.
